BuildMe
Sign InGet Started
Open Source CI/CD Monitor

One dashboard for
all your builds

Monitor GitHub Actions, Travis CI, and CircleCI from a unified dashboard. Real-time build status, environment drift detection, and MCP integration for AI-powered build management.

Start MonitoringSign In
buildme-mcp
$ watch_builds
{ active_builds: 2, poll_interval: 30s }
pingrly/main deploy running 42s
buildme/main test+build queued
$ cancel_build --project 1 --build 847
cancel requested
$ restart_build --project 2 --build 312
retrigger requested

Works with your CI providers

GitHub Actions
Travis CI
CircleCI

Everything you need

From real-time monitoring to AI-powered build management, BuildMe gives you complete visibility into your CI/CD pipelines.

Multi-Provider Dashboard

Unified view of GitHub Actions, Travis CI, and CircleCI builds. Filter by environment, branch, or status.

Real-Time WebSocket

Live build updates pushed via WebSocket. No polling, no refresh — see builds complete in real time.

Environment Drift Detection

Compare deployed SHAs against branch heads. Know instantly when staging or production falls behind.

MCP Integration

AI agents can query builds, cancel runs, restart pipelines, and watch progress through the Model Context Protocol.

Adaptive Polling

30-second polling during active builds, 5-minute intervals when idle. Your AI agent always has fresh data.

Self-Hosted

Single Go binary with embedded frontend and SQLite. Deploy anywhere — your server, your data, your rules.

MCP Server

AI-native build management

The BuildMe MCP server lets AI coding agents interact with your CI/CD pipelines directly. Query build status, cancel runaway builds, restart failed deploys — all through natural language.

  • +watch_builds — adaptive polling with 30s/5min intervals
  • +cancel_build — stop running builds from your editor
  • +restart_build — re-trigger failed deploys instantly
  • +get_build_stats — success rates and failure trends
  • +get_drift — environment version comparison
~/.claude/settings.json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "buildme": {
      "url": "https://mcp.build.biswas.me",
      "headers": {
        "X-API-Key": "bm_..."
      }
    }
  }
}

How it works

1

Connect providers

Add your GitHub, Travis CI, or CircleCI repositories. BuildMe uses API tokens to fetch build data.

2

Monitor builds

See all builds in real time. Environment drift detection compares what's deployed vs. what's built.

3

Act from anywhere

Cancel, restart, or investigate builds from the dashboard or through MCP-connected AI agents.

Ready to monitor?

Self-hosted, open source, and free. Set up in under 5 minutes.

Create Account
BuildMe — Open-source CI/CD build monitor
